description: WIN-011 detail_evidence contains the SAME Wang-2000 vs Yang-Wang-2002 misattribution that was already patched in WIN-012. Current WIN-011 text reads: 'Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D) used a LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter at Mohe during the 1997 total solar eclipse and reported anomalous gravity valleys of 6-7 μGal at first and last contact — critically, not at eclipse maximum. The paper explicitly states "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found."' The phrase 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found' actually appears in the Yang & Wang (2002) Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245 reanalysis, NOT in the Wang et al. (2000) Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101 original paper. The 2000 paper abstract claims a shielding-consistent signal; the 2002 reanalysis (co-authored by the same lead observer Qian-Shen Wang) walks back to 'no significant anomaly.' Verified via ADS WebFetch 2026-04-20 (2002Ap&SS.282..245Y). This creates a citation-integrity inconsistency: WIN-012 cites the quote correctly to Yang & Wang 2002, WIN-011 cites it incorrectly to Wang et al. 2000. A dome defender or fair-minded physicist who fetches the 2000 paper will find no such quote and could accuse us of fabricating a citation. Same error class we criticize the dome for (WIN-008/009/014 had wrong DOIs).
recommended_patch: In WIN-011 detail_evidence, re-attribute the 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found' quote from Wang et al. (2000) to Yang & Wang (2002). Suggested rewrite: 'Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101) used a LaCoste-Romberg D spring gravimeter at Mohe during the 1997 total solar eclipse and reported anomalous gravity valleys of 6-7 μGal at first and last contact — critically, not at eclipse maximum. A subsequent reanalysis co-authored by the same lead observer (Yang & Wang, 2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985) found the evidence weaker than the initial report once additional measurement controls were applied and explicitly states "no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found."' This matches the WIN-012 attribution style introduced in the c3 patch and keeps the two entries citationally consistent.
priority_rationale: Medium-high. The misattribution is in a prominent live section (WIN-011) and creates a cross-WIN inconsistency with the already-patched WIN-012. Every curmudgeon cycle that reviews WIN-011 while WIN-012 is already fixed will flag the same hole. Fix is a targeted 2-3 sentence rewrite with no arithmetic change and no verdict change.
